message.content:
You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer B) for the hc-assay production-hardening campaign, repo at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-8, latest commit). Your job: confirm the fix is BOTH appropriate (correct root cause, sound approach, right layer) AND complete (no residual gap; the regression test actually DISCRIMINATES — fails if the fix is reverted). Be skeptical; try to find a hole.

FINDING K-CI-1:
core (no-extras) lane mypy --strict failed: seam.py's guarded `import httpx` has no stub when the reasoning extra isn't installed. Fix: "httpx.*" added to [[tool.mypy.overrides]] ignore_missing_imports. Test: test_mypy_ignores_httpx_missing_stub_in_core_lane. Verify mypy still passes.

Read the actual changed files and run what you can (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path> — use "python -m pytest", not bare pytest, for your own runs; uv 0.11.21 is available for K-OPS-1). Judge ONLY K-CI-1. Return CONFIRMED only if appropriate AND complete; CONCERN if appropriate but a real residual gap remains (name it); REJECTED if wrong or non-discriminating. List concrete concerns (empty if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll review finding K-CI-1. Let me start by examining the relevant files and the fix.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git log --oneline -8 && echo "---DIFF---" && git show --stat HEAD
input.description: Show recent commits and latest diff stat

bdc2dfc fix(hardening-p7): redact spaced brand persona via separator-flexible handle match (J-008 confirm round 3)
fbb9511 fix(hardening-p7): case-insensitive operator-identity scrub + handle stem (J-002/J-008 confirm round 2)

---DIFF---
commit d08f03147df786fce18af280d398d9b51ff71feb
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 11:22:01 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p8): 8 confirmed findings incl. 3 CI-red blockers — main was failing
    
    Main CI had been RED (bypassed by --admin merges) on THREE counts, plus assessment
    found more. All 2-agent verified (or dispositively confirmed by the red CI logs):
    
    CI-RED (critical path to green):
    - K-CI-2 (high): CI ran the bare `pytest` console script, which (unlike `python -m
      pytest`) does not put the repo root on sys.path → `from tests import ...` failed at
      collection (`No module named 'tests'`); the suite NEVER ran in CI. Fix: pythonpath
      += "." so `tests` imports under any invocation. Now collects 555 tests.
    - K-CI-1 (high): core (no-extras) lane mypy failed on seam.py's guarded `import httpx`
      (httpx ships with the reasoning extra). Fix: httpx.* in mypy ignore_missing_imports.
    - K-OPS-1 (high): lockfile "reproducibility" gate re-resolved against live PyPI (no
      --exclude-newer), going red whenever any in-range transitive published — it was
      failing on clean checkouts. Fix: pin resolution via --exclude-newer sourced from a
      single committed date file (.uv-exclude-newer), in CI + regenerate_lockfiles.sh.
      Chose a cutoff (2026-06-14) clear of a release straddling the boundary (arize-phoenix
      17.6/17.7) where uv resolution was non-deterministic; regenerated all three locks.
    
    CODE (assessment survivors, 2-agent verified true-positive):
    - K-REL-1 (high): SIGTERM trace-flush handler swallowed termination when prior was
      SIG_DFL (the default) → process unkillable by docker stop/kill until SIGKILL. Fix:
      re-establish SIG_DFL and re-deliver the signal so default-terminate still runs.
    - K-OBS-2 (high): provenance artifact temp file leaked + artifact silently dropped when
      a payload held a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes/datetime). Fix: serialize up front
      with default=str (always assigns path → cleanup reachable; artifact attaches); warn in
      track() so a tracker failure is no longer silent.
    - K-SEC-1 (med): local-URI guard rejected Unix-domain-socket Postgres hosts (leading
      '/'), the most data-sovereign connection. Fix: accept a single leading-'/' socket path,
      keep UNC (//, /\) rejection.
    
    OPS:
    - K-OPS-2 (med): regenerate_lockfiles.sh omitted requirements-build.lock (which CI gates).
      Fix: regenerate all three; point CI errors at the script.
    - K-OPS-3 (med): coverage measured but never enforced. Fix: --cov-fail-under=90 (current ~96.6%).
    
    Regression tests added for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean;
    555 passed, 5 skipped; coverage 96.58% (floor 90). Lockfiles reproducible (script == CI regen).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

pyproject.toml:131:    # httpx ships with the reasoning extra; seam.py imports it lazily/guarded for the BULK-tier
pyproject.toml:133:    "httpx.*",

110	# No fixed python_version: mypy infers the target from the running interpreter, so the CI
111	# matrix (3.11–3.14) type-checks against each version rather than only 3.11 (#121).
112	packages = ["assay_engine"]
113	strict = true
114	
115	# Optional reasoning/observability backends are not installed in the type-check env; their
116	# imports are lazy and guarded at runtime, so treat them as untyped third-party modules.
117	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
118	module = [
119	    "langchain_ollama.*",
120	    "claude_agent_sdk.*",
121	    "anyio.*",
122	    "opentelemetry.*",
123	    "phoenix.*",
124	    "mlflow.*",
125	    "openinference.*",
126	    "langgraph.*",
127	    "qdrant_client.*",
128	    "psycopg.*",
129	    "psycopg_pool.*",
130	    "numpy.*",
131	    # httpx ships with the reasoning extra; seam.py imports it lazily/guarded for the BULK-tier
132	    # HTTP timeout, so the no-extras core lane has no stub and mypy --strict failed there (#K-CI-1).
133	    "httpx.*",
134	]
135	ignore_missing_imports = true
136

    - K-CI-1 (high): core (no-extras) lane mypy failed on seam.py's guarded `import httpx`
      (httpx ships with the reasoning extra). Fix: httpx.* in mypy ignore_missing_imports.
    - K-OPS-1 (high): lockfile "reproducibility" gate re-resolved against live PyPI (no

    - K-OBS-2 (high): provenance artifact temp file leaked + artifact silently dropped when
      a payload held a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes/datetime). Fix: serialize up front
      with default=str (always assigns path → cleanup reachable; artifact attaches); warn in
      track() so a tracker failure is no longer silent.
    - K-SEC-1 (med): local-URI guard rejected Unix-domain-socket Postgres hosts (leading
      '/'), the most data-sovereign connection. Fix: accept a single leading-'/' socket path,
      keep UNC (//, /\) rejection.
    
    OPS:
    - K-OPS-2 (med): regenerate_lockfiles.sh omitted requirements-build.lock (which CI gates).

diff --git a/pyproject.toml b/pyproject.toml
index 98bc9a7..8fff93c 100644
--- a/pyproject.toml
+++ b/pyproject.toml
@@ -81,7 +81,12 @@ packages = ["src/assay_engine"]
 
 [tool.pytest.ini_options]
 testpaths = ["tests"]
-pythonpath = ["src"]
+# "src" puts the package on the path; "." (the rootdir) puts the repo root on sys.path so the
+# `tests` package itself is importable (several suites do `from tests import reference_study` and
+# `from tests.integration.conftest import ...`). Without it, the bare `pytest` console script CI
+# runs — unlike `python -m pytest`, which prepends CWD — fails with `ModuleNotFoundError: No
+# module named 'tests'`, so the whole CI test step errored at collection (#K-CI-2).
+pythonpath = ["src", "."]

content.text: Now let me look at the actual seam.py httpx usage and the regression test.

input.description: Find httpx usage in src and seam.py

content.content:
tests/test_supply_chain.py:370:def test_mypy_ignores_httpx_missing_stub_in_core_lane():
tests/test_supply_chain.py:371:    # #K-CI-1: seam.py imports httpx lazily (guarded) for the BULK-tier HTTP timeout; httpx ships
tests/test_supply_chain.py:373:    # failed there. httpx must be in the ignore-missing-imports override list like the other
tests/test_supply_chain.py:380:    assert "httpx.*" in modules, "httpx not in mypy ignore_missing_imports — core lane breaks"

src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:352:    # A bare float sets EVERY httpx phase (connect/read/write/pool) to BULK_TIMEOUT, so a slow
src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:355:    # and aligns with the outer headroom. Fall back to the float if httpx is absent.
src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:357:        import httpx
src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:359:        _bulk_timeout: Any = httpx.Timeout(
src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:362:    except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - httpx ships with the reasoning extra
src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:385:        # Prefer the typed HTTP status (ollama.ResponseError / httpx carry one): 404 = the model
---SEAM---
./src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py

348	
349	    # BULK's INNER bound (#G-003): the sync-tier counterpart to HIGH_STAKES's anyio.fail_after.
350	    # A ThreadPoolExecutor worker blocked in client.invoke() cannot be interrupted by the outer
351	    # _with_timeout, so the HTTP client timeout is what actually frees the slot on a hung backend.
352	    # A bare float sets EVERY httpx phase (connect/read/write/pool) to BULK_TIMEOUT, so a slow
353	    # connect THEN a slow read could total ~2*BULK_TIMEOUT — past the outer BULK_TIMEOUT+10 bound,
354	    # leaking the slot. Use an explicit Timeout so the TOTAL stays ~BULK_TIMEOUT (+ a small connect)
355	    # and aligns with the outer headroom. Fall back to the float if httpx is absent.
356	    try:
357	        import httpx
358	
359	        _bulk_timeout: Any = httpx.Timeout(
360	            BULK_TIMEOUT, connect=min(10.0, BULK_TIMEOUT), pool=min(10.0, BULK_TIMEOUT)
361	        )
362	    except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - httpx ships with the reasoning extra
363	        _bulk_timeout = BULK_TIMEOUT
364	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = dict(
365	        model=model,
366	        base_url=BULK_BASE_URL,
367	        temperature=temperature,

350	
351	
352	def test_sbom_is_emitted():
353	    # #147: a CycloneDX SBOM artifact is produced for downstream license/transitive monitoring.
354	    text = _ci_text()
355	    assert "cyclonedx-json" in text and "sbom" in text.lower()
356	
357	
358	def test_pytest_path_includes_repo_root_so_tests_package_imports():
359	    # #K-CI-2: CI runs the bare `pytest` console script which (unlike `python -m pytest`) does NOT
360	    # prepend CWD to sys.path, so `from tests import ...` failed at collection with
361	    # ModuleNotFoundError and the whole CI test step errored. The pytest pythonpath must include
362	    # the repo root (".") so the `tests` package imports regardless of invocation.
363	    import tomllib
364	
365	    pp = tomllib.loads((_ROOT / "pyproject.toml").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
366	    paths = pp["tool"]["pytest"]["ini_options"]["pythonpath"]
367	    assert "." in paths, "repo root not on pytest pythonpath — bare `pytest` cannot import `tests`"
368	
369	
370	def test_mypy_ignores_httpx_missing_stub_in_core_lane():
371	    # #K-CI-1: seam.py imports httpx lazily (guarded) for the BULK-tier HTTP timeout; httpx ships
372	    # only with the reasoning extra, so the dependency-free core lane has no stub and mypy --strict
373	    # failed there. httpx must be in the ignore-missing-imports override list like the other
374	    # optional backends.
375	    import tomllib
376	
377	    pp = tomllib.loads((_ROOT / "pyproject.toml").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
378	    overrides = pp["tool"]["mypy"]["overrides"]
379	    modules = {m for o in overrides if o.get("ignore_missing_imports") for m in o.get("module", [])}
380	    assert "httpx.*" in modules, "httpx not in mypy ignore_missing_imports — core lane breaks"
381	
382	
383	def test_coverage_floor_is_enforced():
384	    # #K-OPS-3: `pytest --cov` without a threshold exits 0 at any coverage, so the gate was vacuous.

content.text: Now let me verify the test discriminates and that mypy actually passes in the core lane. Let me check the CI to understand what the "core lane" mypy invocation is.

content.content:
15:  # Dependency-free core: the engine must lint, type-check, and test with NO extras installed
17:  core:
29:          # Install the dev toolchain from the hashed, audited core lockfile so tests run against
32:          # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
33:          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
34:          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
35:          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
36:          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
41:      - name: Type-check (mypy)
42:        run: mypy
46:  # Extras lane: install the real backends and run the integration suite so the backend code
47:  # paths actually execute, and mypy type-checks against the REAL (not ignored) library types.
79:          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
80:          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
81:          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
82:          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
83:      - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
84:        run: mypy
114:  # (requirements.lock) so the audit is reproducible across runs and matches what installs
129:        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip && python -m pip install pip-audit==2.10.1
135:          python -m pip install uv==0.11.21
147:          # the core lockfile (dev extra only) backs the hash-pinned core lane (#F-004) and must
149:          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
150:          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
151:            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh"; exit 1; }
153:          # editable install (#J-003) and must stay in sync.
207:          # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
209:          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
210:          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
211:          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
212:          python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
226:  # silently dropped from the merge gate by requiring just `core`.
229:    needs: [core, integration, audit]
234:          results="${{ needs.core.result }} ${{ needs.integration.result }} ${{ needs.audit.result }}"

content.text: mypy passes and the test passes. Now the critical discrimination checks: (1) Does the test fail if the fix is reverted? (2) Does mypy actually fail without the override when httpx is absent (proving the override is the real fix, not something else)?

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && cp pyproject.toml /tmp/pyproject.bak
# Revert the fix: remove httpx.* line
python - <<'EOF'
import re
p="pyproject.toml"
s=open(p).read()
s=s.replace('    # httpx ships with the reasoning extra; seam.py imports it lazily/guarded for the BULK-tier\n    # HTTP timeout, so the no-extras core lane has no stub and mypy --strict failed there (#K-CI-1).\n    "httpx.*",\n','')
open(p,"w").write(s)
EOF
echo "=== test with fix reverted ==="
python -m pytest -q tests/test_supply_chain.py::test_mypy_ignores_httpx_missing_stub_in_core_lane 2>&1 | tail -5
cp /tmp/pyproject.bak pyproject.toml
echo "=== restored ==="

content.text: Test discriminates. Now the deeper check: does the override actually fix the mypy error in the core lane (httpx absent)? Since my venv HAS httpx, mypy might pass even without the override. I need to simulate httpx being absent to confirm the override is genuinely necessary and sufficient. Let me make httpx unimportable to mypy and test both with and without the override.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate
# Hide httpx so mypy sees it as missing (simulate core lane). Temporarily move the package out.
HX=$(python -c "import os,httpx; print(os.path.dirname(httpx.__file__))")
echo "httpx dir: $HX"
mv "$HX" "${HX}.hidden"
# also hide dist-info to be safe? mypy uses module resolution on sys.path; moving package suffices.
echo "=== mypy WITH override (httpx hidden) ==="
python -m mypy 2>&1 | tail -3
echo "exit=${PIPESTATUS[0]}"
echo "=== now remove override and re-run (httpx still hidden) ==="
cp pyproject.toml /tmp/pyproject.bak2
python - <<'EOF'
p="pyproject.toml"; s=open(p).read()
s=s.replace('    "httpx.*",\n','')
open(p,"w").write(s)
EOF
python -m mypy 2>&1 | grep -i "httpx\|error\|success" | tail -8
echo "exit=${PIPESTATUS[0]}"
# restore everything
cp /tmp/pyproject.bak2 pyproject.toml
mv "${HX}.hidden" "$HX"
echo "=== restored httpx and pyproject ==="

content.text:
Everything restored cleanly. My analysis is complete.

Summary of verification:

**Root cause correctly identified.** seam.py:357 has a guarded `import httpx` (with `except ImportError` fallback) used for the BULK-tier `httpx.Timeout`. httpx ships only with the reasoning extra and is absent from `requirements-core.lock`, so in the no-extras core CI lane mypy `--strict` raised `Cannot find implementation or library stub for module named "httpx" [import-not-found]`.

**Fix is appropriate.** Adding `"httpx.*"` to the existing `[[tool.mypy.overrides]]` block with `ignore_missing_imports = true` is the exact same pattern already applied to the other optional/guarded backends (langchain_ollama, claude_agent_sdk, etc.). Right layer (mypy config, not a code hack), sound approach.

**Discrimination proven dispositively** by simulating the actual failing condition (the full dev venv had httpx, masking the bug). With httpx physically hidden:
- WITH override: `Success: no issues found in 40 source files`
- WITHOUT override: `seam.py:357: error: Cannot find implementation or library stub for module named "httpx"` — exactly the K-CI-1 failure.

So the override is both necessary and sufficient to fix the real error in the core lane.

**Regression test discriminates:** `test_mypy_ignores_httpx_missing_stub_in_core_lane` passes as-is and FAILS when the `httpx.*` line is removed.

**Minor concern (not blocking):** The regression test is a static assertion that `"httpx.*"` is present in the override list — it does not invoke mypy with httpx absent, so it would not catch a future scenario where mypy semantics change or httpx leaks into the core lock. This is a fidelity gap, but it is consistent with the other supply-chain tests in this file (they parse config too), it correctly couples to this specific fix, and a behavioral mypy-in-isolation test would be heavy/flaky for CI. Acceptable.

All state (httpx package, pyproject.toml) restored to original.

input.rationale: Root cause is correctly identified: seam.py:357 has a guarded `import httpx` (used for the BULK-tier httpx.Timeout, with an ImportError float fallback). httpx ships only with the reasoning extra and is confirmed absent from requirements-core.lock (the no-extras core CI lane), so bare `mypy --strict` there raised `import-not-found` for httpx. The fix — adding "httpx.*" to the existing [[tool.mypy.overrides]] ignore_missing_imports block — is at the right layer and follows the identical established pattern used for the other optional/guarded backends (langchain_ollama, claude_agent_sdk, opentelemetry, etc.). I proved the fix is both necessary and sufficient by simulating the real core-lane condition: my dev venv contained httpx (masking the bug), so I physically hid the httpx package and re-ran mypy. WITH the override: 'Success: no issues found in 40 source files'. WITHOUT the override (httpx still hidden): the exact K-CI-1 error `seam.py:357: error: Cannot find implementation or library stub for module named "httpx" [import-not-found]`. The regression test passes as-is and fails when the httpx.* line is reverted, so it discriminates. All temporary state (httpx package, pyproject.toml) was restored and verified clean.
concerns: The regression test is a static config-parse assertion (checks `httpx.*` is in the ignore list); it does not actually invoke mypy with httpx absent. It correctly couples to this fix and fails on revert, but it would not catch a future regression where httpx leaks into the core lock or mypy import semantics change. This matches the style of the sibling supply-chain tests and is acceptable, not blocking.

content.text: Verified K-CI-1 — CONFIRMED. The fix is appropriate and complete, with the discrimination proven dispositively by simulating httpx absence.
